source:
gantt
dateFormat yyyy-mm-dd
title Adding gantt diagram functionality to mermaid
excludes weekdays 2019-02-01
A task :a1, 2019-02-04, 3d
upstreamBehavior: The grammar accepts 'weekdays' (a token the docs explicitly say is unsupported); isInvalidDate never matches it, so it is silently ignored and the chart renders.
notes: Spec-mandated divergence (docs/design/families/gantt.md: fail clearly for unsupported syntax instead of silently mis-rendering). The agent layer still parses, round-trips, and reports verify UNRESOLVABLE_SCHEDULE. NB: the task line was added here to give the schedule something to resolve; upstream's original fixture is directive-only.

source:
gantt
dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
section testa1
test1 :id1,2013-01-01,2w
test2 :id2,after id3,1d
upstreamBehavior: Unknown 'after' refs fall back to wall-clock today at local midnight — the expected value in their table is literally new Date(new Date().setHours(0,0,0,0)).
notes: Determinism contract: named error instead of a date that changes every day.

upstream: should add 1 day even on days with 25 hours
source:
gantt
dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
section Task handles 25 hour day
daylight savings day :id1,2020-11-01,1d
upstreamBehavior: Runs only under TZ=America/Los_Angeles (their suite skips it otherwise) and asserts a 25-hour day across the DST fall-back.
notes: Our scheduler is UTC-only: every day is 24h by construction, so the bug class this test guards against cannot exist. The source itself resolves fine for us (2020-11-01 → 2020-11-02 UTC).

upstream: should handle seconds-only format with valid numeric values (issue #5496)
source:
gantt
dateFormat ss
section Network Request
RTT :rtt, 0, 20
upstreamBehavior: Start '0' fails dayjs strict 'ss' parsing (needs two digits) and falls back to new Date(); the test only asserts the task exists.
notes: Write '00' and the format resolves deterministically in our implementation.

upstream: should handle dates with year typo like 202 instead of 2024 (issue #5496)
source:
gantt
dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
section Vacation
London Trip 1 :2024-12-01, 7d
London Trip 2 :202-12-01, 7d
upstreamBehavior: The '202-12-01' typo falls through dayjs strict parsing to new Date('202-12-01') and schedules a task in the year 202.
notes: A loud error beats a silent 1,800-year scheduling mistake for agent workflows.

source:
gantt
dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
section testa1
test1 :id1,2013-01-01,1f
upstreamBehavior: '1f' parses to a NaN duration; the end date falls back through invalid-date handling.
notes: '1f' is not a duration token, so we read it as an end DATE and reject it by name (the spec's matrix cites mermaid #6586 for invalid duration tokens).

upstream: frontmatter/compact docs example: 'data entry ... : 1s   %% not yet official'
source:
gantt
dateFormat YYYY
title Inline comment tolerance
entry :a, 2024, 1s   %% not yet official
upstreamBehavior: Mermaid's taskData lexer rule ([^#:\n]+) consumes the inline '%% …' text INTO the metadata; '1s   %% not yet official' then fails their duration regex and the end date falls back to wall-clock today. The official docs example only renders because of that fallback.
notes: Mermaid comments are documented as own-line only; we neither invent inline-comment grammar nor substitute today. The agent layer still round-trips the line byte-faithfully, and verify names the problem via UNRESOLVABLE_SCHEDULE.
